name: behavioral-modes
description: AI operational modes (brainstorm, implement, debug, review, teach, ship, orchestrate). Use to adapt behavior based on task type.
capabilities: Automatically detect appropriate mode from user request keywords
Switch between divergent brainstorming and focused implementation modes
Provide structured debug analysis with root cause identification
Generate categorized code review feedback by severity
Create educational explanations with analogies and examples
Support multi-agent collaboration patterns for complex tasks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 34 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is pure documentation describing behavioral modes with markdown-formatted output examples. No executable code, shell commands, or cryptographic operations exist. The skill only uses read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep) and provides behavioral guidance for AI interactions.
valueStatement: Generic AI responses waste time and miss task-specific needs. This skill provides seven behavioral modes that optimize AI output for brainstorming, implementation, debugging, review, teaching, shipping, and multi-agent collaboration.
userTitle: Adapt AI Behavior with Seven Operational Modes
limitations: Mode detection relies on keyword matching and may require explicit mode requests
Behavioral guidance only affects AI output style, not actual tool capabilities
Does not execute code or perform automated testing
Multi-agent patterns require external orchestration tools

useCases.title: Development Team Lead
useCases.description: Use DEBUG mode for systematic bug investigation and REVIEW mode for thorough pull request analysis with severity-categorized feedback.

useCases.title: Product Designer
useCases.description: Use BRAINSTORM mode to explore multiple feature alternatives with visual diagrams before committing to implementation direction.

useCases.title: Learning Developer
useCases.description: Use TEACH mode for concept explanations with analogies and exercises, then IMPLEMENT mode for production-ready code examples.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Mode Activation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use BRAINSTORM mode to generate three different approaches for implementing user authentication in my React app.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Explicitly requesting a specific mode

promptTemplates.title: Debug Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: My login API returns 401 errors even with correct credentials. Use DEBUG mode to investigate: check the auth middleware, trace the token validation flow, and identify the root cause.

promptTemplates.title: Code Review Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this pull request using REVIEW mode. Categorize findings by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and provide improved code examples for each issue.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Agent PEC Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the PLAN-EXECUTE-CRITIC pattern: First create a task breakdown in task.md, then implement the feature using IMPLEMENT mode, finally perform security and performance review using REVIEW mode.

bestPractices: Explicitly request a mode when the task type is clear to ensure optimal AI behavior
Combine BRAINSTORM mode early with IMPLEMENT mode later for structured workflow
Use SHIP mode checklist before any production deployment
antiPatterns: Using IMPLEMENT mode for open-ended exploration that needs BRAINSTORM mode
Skipping REVIEW mode when deploying security-sensitive features
Expecting mode switching to bypass AI safety guidelines or tool limitations

faq.question: How does automatic mode detection work?
faq.answer: The skill matches keywords in your request to trigger words. For example, 'ideas' or 'what if' triggers BRAINSTORM mode, while 'error' or 'not working' triggers DEBUG mode. You can always explicitly request a mode using /mode syntax.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple modes in one session?
faq.answer: Yes. A common pattern is BRAINSTORM for ideation, then IMPLEMENT for coding, then REVIEW for quality checks. Each request can use a different mode based on your current task phase.

faq.question: What happens if the wrong mode is detected?
faq.answer: Simply restate your request with an explicit mode prefix like '/debug' or 'Use DEBUG mode'. The explicit request overrides automatic detection.

faq.question: Do modes affect which tools the AI can use?
faq.answer: No. Modes only change communication style, output format, and prioritization. Tool availability depends on the skill's allowed-tools configuration, not the active mode.

faq.question: Is TEACH mode suitable for production code generation?
faq.answer: TEACH mode prioritizes educational clarity over production optimization. Use IMPLEMENT mode for production code, then TEACH mode separately if you need explanation of the generated code.

faq.question: How does the multi-agent PEC pattern work?
faq.answer: PEC (Plan-Execute-Critic) sequences three modes: Planner decomposes tasks into steps, Executor implements using IMPLEMENT mode, and Critic reviews using REVIEW mode. This pattern improves quality on complex tasks.
